title: N.H. State Police announce unexpected death of 19-year veteran
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sgt. Brian J. Ross, a 19-year veteran of New Hampshire State Police, died unexpectedly at age 41 on Monday.
Ross served roles in Major Crime Unit, Motorcycle Unit, and led the Collision Analysis and Reconstruction Unit.
A fund supports his family, including his wife and three children; services are scheduled for July 6-7 in Hampton.

title: Jaylen Brown says Celtics showed ‘lack of respect’ after trade to Sixers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown expressed dissatisfaction with the communication from Celtics' management during his trade to the 76ers.
Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to the 76ers for Paul George and four draft picks, earning mixed reviews.
Brown stated he felt disrespected by the lack of explanation and communication regarding his trade from Boston.

title: Hull man dies after apparent drowning at Nantasket Beach, officials say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Robert Nolan, 76, from Hull, died after an apparent drowning at Nantasket Beach on Wednesday evening.
Authorities responded to a report of an unresponsive man in the water; Nolan was pronounced dead at 8:40 p.m.
The Office of the Chief Medical Examiner will perform an examination; no foul play is suspected, officials say.

title: Don Sweeney, Bruins’ retooling plans remain a work in progress
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins traded goalie Joonas Korpisalo to the Rangers for cap relief and acquired defenseman Will Borgen.
Boston made strategic moves by re-signing Connor Clifton and adding JJ Peterka to enhance their lineup.
With $5.3 million in cap space, Bruins may trade players to streamline their roster before the season.

title: Parking will be free at state parks, beaches for Fourth of July weekend
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Parking fees waived at all Massachusetts state parks and beaches from July 3 to July 5.
Free parking offer commemorates the 250th anniversary of the American Revolution.
Some North Shore beaches closed due to sewer issues; check DCR Park Alerts for updates.

title: What’s up with those online ads for gas-saving gadgets?
author: John Paul
bullets: John Paul, AAA Northeast's Car Doctor, warns against unverified claims of gas-saving gadgets like pills, air filters, and magnets.
Car Doctor advises against purchasing unnecessary gadgets, recommending instead to drive efficiently by maintaining speed limits and combining trips.
Online ads often promote gas-saving devices with no proven effectiveness, sometimes claiming unnamed NASA scientist origins.

title: Boston civilian board recommends changes to BPD policies on bodycam footage, federal entanglements
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston's Office of Police Accountability and Transparency (OPAT) recommends updating bodycam footage release policies after recent police shootings.
OPAT urges Boston Police to decouple from federal agencies like FBI and DHS, citing concerns over civil rights and data sharing.
Recommendations include tighter biometric data sharing controls and quarterly reports to City Council on civil immigration detainer responses.

title: Senators seek to restrict social media use for minors, target ‘most addictive features’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts Senate advances bill to limit addictive features on social media for minors, bypassing controversial age verification.
Bill proposes disabling features like autoplay and infinite scroll for minors, with reminders after one hour of use.
Privacy enhancements include limiting direct messaging and geolocation sharing for minors, with option to adjust settings.

title: Isaiah Thomas comes to Jaylen Brown’s defense after shocking Celtics trade​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown was traded from the Boston Celtics to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George and draft picks.
Former teammate Isaiah Thomas defended Brown on social media, emphasizing Brown's character amid criticism.
Reports suggest the Celtics valued Derrick White's season over Brown's, despite Brown's MVP-level performance.

title: Jeremiah Poope takes us on the Freedom Trail for America’s 250th (yes, that’s really his name)
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Jeremiah Poope, a 23-year veteran tour guide, leads Freedom Trail tours in period costume despite a Boston heat wave.
The Worcester-area native uses personal anecdotes and humor to engage visitors on the 2.5-mile historical walk.
Poope's unique approach includes sharing lesser-known Revolutionary figures to make history relatable to children.
